## Summary

<!-- What changed and why? -->

## Authoring Checklist

- [ ] I started with a live source sweep before drafting or revising.
- [ ] I prioritized official pages, author-maintained notes/books, venue pages, or primary papers.
- [ ] I filled the required front matter fields for the page type.
- [ ] I updated `last-reviewed`, `source-check-date`, `review-cadence`, and `update-status`.
- [ ] I included at least one primary source, one second perspective, and one paper/application bridge.
- [ ] I kept the required section order for the page type.
- [ ] I checked prerequisite, next, and related links.
- [ ] If the page includes code, simulation, or visualization, I documented what it shows and how it renders on the static site.
- [ ] If the page includes randomness or numerics, I documented seeds, key parameters, or numerical cautions.
- [ ] I used `draft: true` for unfinished work instead of publishing a thin page.
- [ ] I rendered locally with `quarto render` or expect CI to render cleanly.
